header.location: Portland, OR (Open to Remote or Relocation)

summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Human Resources professional who thrives on keeping HRIS data accurate and operations running smoothly, developing people programs grounded in insights, and collaborating with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve employee experience—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

Manage 20+ programs and 10+ vendors, refining program positioning and {coord|communications strategy} via {data|feedback analysis}
Supported HRBP team by piloting an Inclusive Leadership workshop for senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Facilitated smooth {coord|organizational change} by directing a Mergers & Acquisitions project, overseeing {cross|cross-functional stakeholders}
Streamlined {ex|onboarding} processes across 3 global locations by conducting gap analyses and clarifying job responsibilities

Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming {data|feedback data} into actionable reports that informed {ex|engagement} strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences to amplify employee voice and guide C-suite decisions on satisfaction drivers
Improved {ex|employee experience} via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and {prog|leadership training}

changeLog.note: Tailored for Roivant Sciences People Operations Associate (biotech/holding company). Summary updated to emphasize HRIS data accuracy and operational excellence. InGenius bullets: Set H emphasis — cross-functional processes, documentation, data accuracy, onboarding 75% reduction (key anchor metric), program management scale. Vestas: HR variant — HRBP support, M&A project (maps to holding company integration), onboarding optimization, employee relations compliance. Skills: added AI Tools (ChatGPT, Notion AI) per JD's AI tools emphasis; kept Greenhouse (exact match) and SAP as HRIS signals. Kronos trimmed to 2 bullets for page fit. NextGen retained 3 bullets for OD depth.
